name: Broadsheet Analysis
summary: Editorial research system with dense paper grids, serif display, mono kickers, hard rules, and analytical story hierarchy.

style_dna.philosophy: Analysis should feel edited, argued, and typeset, not merely summarized.
style_dna.visual_signature: Paper-white field, serif headlines, mono uppercase kickers, hairline rules, dense story modules.
style_dna.surface_logic: Flat print surfaces; depth comes from rule weight and typographic hierarchy.

palette.accent_policy: Use one blue for links, references, or the strongest analytical highlight.
palette.semantic_policy: Prefer labels and rules to traffic-light colors.

layout.grid: Magazine grid, columns, numbered lists, pull quotes, and section ribbons.
layout.whitespace: Dense but disciplined; whitespace acts like page margin.
layout.image_treatment: Hard-cropped editorial images or charts with captions.

components.buttons: Hard-outline rectangles or underlined text links.
components.cards: No floating cards; use story blocks separated by rules.
components.tables: Report-style tables with hairline dividers and strong headers.
components.callouts: Pull quotes, source notes, and numbered evidence blocks.

slides.accent_rule: Use mono kickers, hairline dividers, and one ink-blue accent.

document.heading_rule: Serif display, mono kickers, hairline rules, dense evidence blocks.

source_inheritance.method: Synthesized from local getdesign.md working corpus.

do: Prefer pull quotes, sources, and numbered evidence.
dont: Do not use rounded SaaS cards.
Do not add glow or glass effects.
Do not make editorial layouts too sparse.

pptx: Use deterministic HTML/CSS or PPTX text boxes for final text.
Keep one core idea per slide and validate rendered PNG dimensions.
Use tables, numbered evidence, and source notes instead of vague decorative cards.
docx: Translate visual style into Word typography, margins, heading hierarchy, tables, and callouts.
Keep documents printable unless the user explicitly requests PDF-only visual art direction.
carousel: Use high-resolution fixed canvas, mobile-readable text, and safe margins.
Make each frame complete enough to stand alone.
